Class WindowsElement

java.lang.Object
io.github.sentinel.elements.Element
io.github.sentinel.elements.WindowsElement
public class WindowsElement extends Element
WinAppDriver implementation of a Element.

    Windows does not support color information, as such this is an unsupported operation for windows elements.

    Returns the Color of the pixel one below and one to the right of the top left pixel of the element.

    element(org.openqa.selenium.By locator)
    Returns a Selenium WebElement using the current element as the beginning search point for the passed By locator.

    existsAtThisInstant()
    Performs exactly 1 sweep across the DOM for the element (includes all selectors).

    Searches for the current element within the current frame context.

    Searches recursively through any iFrames on the page for the element.

    getBy()
    Returns the first stored selector for this element.

    Returns a Point object that represents the top-left corner of the element.

    getMostPrevalentColor()
    Returns the most prevalent color present in the element.

    getName()
    Returns the name of the element as it is stored in the yaml file.
    String
    getTooltipText()
    Returns the tooltip value of the element.
    boolean
    hasAttribute(String attribute)
    Returns true if the attribute exists for the element; otherwise returns false.
    Element
    hover()
    Hovers over an element using Actions.
    boolean
    isDisabled()
    Returns true if the element is disabled or readonly; false otherwise Expects the element to be disabled, and if it is not, this method will check every 10 milliseconds until it is up to the configured timeout time (10 second default).
    boolean
    isDisplayed()
    Returns true if the element is displayed, otherwise returns false if it is hidden/invisible.
    boolean
    isEnabled()
    Returns true if the element is enabled and doesn't have the attribute readonly; false otherwise.
    boolean
    isHidden()
    Returns true if the element is hidden, otherwise returns false if it is visible/displayed.
    boolean
    isNotSelected()
    Returns true if the element is not selected; false if it is.
    boolean
    isSelected()
    Returns true if the element is selected; false if it is not.
    Element
    sendFilePaths(List<String> fileLocators)
    Sends a constructed collection of absolute paths of a given file locator string list to an element for use with file upload elements.
    Element
    sendSpecialKey(org.openqa.selenium.Keys key)
    Sends a unicode character to the element directly.
    Boolean
    waitForText(String text, boolean present)
    Waits until the text contains a certain value, and returns if it was found

  • Constructor Details

    • WindowsElement

      public WindowsElement(String elementName, Map<String,String> selectors)
      Default Windows Element constructor. An override of the Element constructor.
      Parameters:
      elementName - String the name of the element
      selectors - Map<String,String> the list of selectors to use to find the element

  • Method Details

    • doesNotExist

      public boolean doesNotExist()
      Returns true if an element is neither found nor displayed otherwise false. Will poll every selector on the page object in a loop until the timeout is reached. This should be used when you expect an element to not be present and do not want to slow down your tests waiting for the normal timeout time to expire. Defaults to assuming iframes will never exist for windows elements, and may exist for web elements.
      Overrides:
      doesNotExist in class Element
      Returns:
      boolean true if the element cannot be found, false if it is found

    • element

      protected org.openqa.selenium.WebElement element()
      Returns the Selenium WebElement if it can be found on the current page. Provides late binding for elements so that the driver does not look for them until they are called, at which point the driver should be on the correct page.
      Overrides:
      element in class Element
      Returns:
      org.openqa.selenium.WebElement the Selenium WebElement object type that can be acted upon

    • createByLocator

      protected org.openqa.selenium.By createByLocator(SelectorType selectorType, String selectorValue)
      Takes a Sentinel SelectorType and string value and returns a Selenium By locator specifically for Windows elements.

      NOTE: TagName (LocalizedControlType) is unsupported at this time for windows element identification. This seems to be an appium issue.

      Overrides:
      createByLocator in class Element
      Parameters:
      selectorType - SelectorType the ENUM value indicating how we search for the element
      selectorValue - String the value being pulled from the config file for the selector
      Returns:
      org.openqa.selenium.By returns a Selenium By selector for locating an element.

    • sendKeys

      public Element sendKeys(String text)
      Enter text into a Element. Typically used for text boxes. This method will throw an ElementDisabledException() if the text box is disabled.
      Overrides:
      sendKeys in class Element
      Parameters:
      text - String the text to enter
      Returns:
      Element (for chaining)

    • getText

      public String getText()
      Returns the text of the page element as a String.
      Overrides:
      getText in class Element
      Returns:
      String the text value stored in the element

    • clear

      public Element clear()
      Clear an Element. Clears text in a text box. If the call to selenium's clear() fails to actually remove text, this method will send CTRL+A + BACKSPACE key chord to attempt to clear the element text.
      Overrides:
      clear in class Element
      Returns:
      Element (for chaining)

    • click

      public Element click()
      Click an Element.
      Overrides:
      click in class Element
      Returns:
      Element (for chaining)

    • getColorAtOffset

      public org.openqa.selenium.support.Color getColorAtOffset(int xOffset, int yOffset)
      Returns the Color of the pixel at the given xOffset, yOffset relative to the top left pixel of the element. WARNING!!! Windows (OS) setting "Make everything bigger" (app and text scaling) must be set to 100% for proper use of this method.
      Parameters:
      xOffset - int the pixels to the right of the left edge of the element
      yOffset - int the pixels below the top edge of the element
      Returns:
      Color the color at the specified pixel offset in the element.

    • getColorAtOffset

      public org.openqa.selenium.support.Color getColorAtOffset()
      Returns the Color of the pixel one below and one to the right of the top left pixel of the element. WARNING!!! Windows (OS) setting "Make everything bigger" (app and text scaling) must be set to 100% for proper use of this method.
      Returns:
      Color the color of the pixel one below and one to the right of the top left pixel of the element.

    • attributeEquals

      public boolean attributeEquals(String attribute, String value)
      Returns true if the element has an attribute equal to the value passed; otherwise returns false. For Windows elements, if attribute == "color" the color is checked via screenshot analysis. Otherwise attribute is checked by grabbing attribute off the element.

      Examples:

      • Determine if an element has a style="display:none" attribute set.
      Overrides:
      attributeEquals in class Element
      Parameters:
      attribute - String the attribute to look for
      value - String the value to which attribute should be checked against
      Returns:
      boolean true if the element as an attribute equal to the value passed; otherwise returns false

    • getBackgroundColor

      public Color getBackgroundColor()
      Windows does not support color information, as such this is an unsupported operation for windows elements.
      Overrides:
      getBackgroundColor in class Element
      Returns:
      Color Java color object of the background color of the element

    • getAttribute

      public String getAttribute(String attribute)
      NOTE: In addition to the documentation seen below, if the "color" attribute is used on a windows element it will perform a special return as the "color" attribute does not properly exist as an attribute in windows. Instead, it is calculated in Sentinel using a screenshot and offset.

      From selenium's javadoc:
      Get the value of the given attribute of the element. Will return the current value, even if this has been modified after the page has been loaded.
      More exactly, this method will return the value of the property with the given name, if it exists. If it does not, then the value of the attribute with the given name is returned. If neither exists, null is returned.
      The "style" attribute is converted as best can be to a text representation with a trailingsemi-colon.
      The following are deemed to be "boolean" attributes, and will return either "true" or null: async, autofocus, autoplay, checked, compact, complete, controls, declare, defaultchecked, defaultselected, defer, disabled, draggable, ended, formnovalidate, hidden, indeterminate, iscontenteditable, ismap, itemscope, loop, multiple, muted, nohref, noresize, noshade, novalidate, nowrap, open, paused, pubdate, readonly, required, reversed, scoped, seamless, seeking, selected, truespeed, willvalidate
      Finally, the following commonly mis-capitalized attribute/property names are evaluated as expected:
      •If the given name is "class", the "className" property is returned.
      •If the given name is "readonly", the "readOnly" property is returned. Note: The reason for this behavior is that users frequently confuse attributes and properties. If you need to do something more precise, e.g., refer to an attribute even when aproperty of the same name exists, then you should evaluate Javascript to obtain the resultyou desire.

      Gets the value of the given attribute on this element.
      Overrides:
      getAttribute in class Element
      Parameters:
      attribute - String name of the attribute to get the value of
      Returns:
      String the value of the given attribute, or null if it is not set.

    • getScreenshot

      public File getScreenshot()
      Returns a screenshot File of the current element.
      Overrides:
      getScreenshot in class Element
      Returns:
      File a screenshot of the current element

    • screenshotElement

      private BufferedImage screenshotElement()
      Returns a BufferedImage taken of the current element
      Returns:
      BufferedImage A robot screenshot cropped to the current element
